① Signal
Signal
Play the over 8.5.2 units
High-confidence edge — 78% model conviction at 12.0K vs 8.5 line.
Misiorowski generates genuine swing-and-miss (38.3% WHF, above LG) — stuff can pile up Ks when working. CU is an effective two-strike weapon — batters who get deep in counts are in trouble. CIN is roughly neutral — 25.04% MIX-K close to league average. Moderate variance (σ2.4K) — no major consistency flags.
⚠ Best value at the 8.5 alt line vs the 9.5 market main — be on the right number.
Bet wins if
✓He logs his average 6.6 IP — enough PA to let Ks accumulate
✓CU converts two-strike counts (33% putaway rate) — batters can't put it in play late
✓CIN swings vs his specific mix (26.1% MIX-K) — lineup is genuinely exploitable
Bet loses if
✗Variance on any given start (σ2.4K) — projection is a median, not a floor

High-confidence edge — 74% model conviction at 7.7K vs 5.5 line.
Eovaldi generates genuine swing-and-miss (30.0% WHF, above LG) — stuff can pile up Ks when working. FF is a serviceable finishing pitch, not dominant in two-strike counts. DET is roughly neutral — 25.58% MIX-K close to league average. He's in form — recent trend is a tailwind.
⚠ Best value at the 5.5 alt line vs the 6.5 market main — be on the right number.
Bet wins if
✓He logs his average 6.8 IP — enough PA to let Ks accumulate
✓Sharp command (2.1 BB/9) — every PA counts and he's not giving away bases
✓Elite swing-and-miss stuff (30.0% WHF) — Ks can stack quickly when he's on
Bet loses if
✗FF is serviceable not dominant (24% putaway) — won't blow batters away

① Signal
Signal
Over 4.5 is the play.1 unit
Solid over edge at 5.6K vs 4.5. 63% CAP — standalone play, not a parlay filler.
Martin is above average on whiff rate (27.1%) — solid enough to capitalize when lineup cooperates. Putaway pitch (SL) is below average — converting deep counts to Ks is a genuine weak spot. CLE is a mild headwind — 23.44% MIX-K just below average vs his specific stuff. Moderate variance (σ2.5K) — no major consistency flags.
Bet wins if
✓Model projects 5.6K vs 4.5 line — edge is real at current number
Bet loses if
✗He averages only 5.6 IP — short outings cap K upside hard
✗SL is serviceable not dominant (20% putaway) — won't blow batters away

Over 4.5 is the play.1 unit
Solid over edge at 5.3K vs 4.5. 59% CAP — standalone play, not a parlay filler.
Sasaki is above average on whiff rate (29.2%) — solid enough to capitalize when lineup cooperates. Putaway pitch (SL) is below average — converting deep counts to Ks is a genuine weak spot. SD is a mild headwind — 22.94% MIX-K just below average vs his specific stuff. He's cooled off recently — factor into your confidence.

✓Model projects 5.3K vs 4.5 line — edge is real at current number

✗He averages only 5.4 IP — short outings cap K upside hard

① Signal
Signal
Lean over 2.5 — size down.0.5 unit
Marginal over edge (3.1K vs 2.5), 58% conviction — take it reduced.
Kolek is below average on swing-and-miss (19.0% WHF vs 25% LG) — needs favorable conditions to reach Ks. Putaway pitch (FC) is below average — converting deep counts to Ks is a genuine weak spot. TB is tougher than it looks — MIX-K% drops to 20.88%, below league average vs his specific pitch mix. He's cooled off recently — factor into your confidence.
⚠ Best value at the 2.5 alt line vs the 3.5 market main — be on the right number.
Bet wins if
✓Sharp command (1.9 BB/9) — every PA counts and he's not giving away bases
Bet loses if
✗He averages only 5.8 IP — short outings cap K upside hard
✗Weak finishing pitch — FC at only 18% putaway, batters fight off pitches in deep counts
✗TB makes contact vs his pitch mix (19.5% MIX-K) — tough K environment today

Lean over 4.5 — size down.0.5 unit
Marginal over edge (5.1K vs 4.5), 56% conviction — take it reduced.
May sits around league average on whiff rate (22.2%) — relies more on weak contact than pure swing-and-miss. Putaway pitch (SW) is below average — converting deep counts to Ks is a genuine weak spot. ATL is tougher than it looks — MIX-K% drops to 22.0%, below league average vs his specific pitch mix. He's cooled off recently — factor into your confidence.

✓Model projects 5.1K vs 4.5 line — edge is real at current number

① Signal
Signal
Under 5.5 is the play.1.5 units
Strong under signal — 70% model probability below 5.5 at 4.5K projected.
Jones generates genuine swing-and-miss (31.7% WHF, above LG) — stuff can pile up Ks when working. SL is a serviceable finishing pitch, not dominant in two-strike counts. PHI is a mild headwind — 24.14% MIX-K just below average vs his specific stuff. Very consistent output (σ0.9K) — model projection is reliable here.
⚠ Best value at the 5.5 alt line vs the 4.5 market main — be on the right number.
Bet wins if
✓Short average outing (4.2 IP) — fewer PA limits total K ceiling
Bet loses if
✗K/9 of 9.0 means the ceiling exists — a long outing can push over the number
✗Low FPS (56%) — he works from behind, counts go deep, Ks can build late
✗Above-average whiff stuff (31.7%) — if he's locked in, Ks stack even vs contact lineups

Model projects 3.9K vs 3.5 — edge too thin to take a side.
Cecconi is below average on swing-and-miss (21.3% WHF vs 25% LG) — needs favorable conditions to reach Ks. Putaway pitch (SL) is below average — converting deep counts to Ks is a genuine weak spot. CWS is a mild headwind — 23.86% MIX-K just below average vs his specific stuff. He's cooled off recently — factor into your confidence.
⚠ Best value at the 3.5 alt line vs the 4.5 market main — be on the right number.

✓Model projects 3.9K vs 3.5 line — edge is real at current number
Bet loses if

① Signal
Signal
Pass.Pass
Model projects 2.7K vs 2.5 — edge too thin to take a side.
Vásquez is below average on swing-and-miss (20.9% WHF vs 25% LG) — needs favorable conditions to reach Ks. Putaway pitch (SL) is below average — converting deep counts to Ks is a genuine weak spot. LAD is tougher than it looks — MIX-K% drops to 22.18%, below league average vs his specific pitch mix. Moderate variance (σ1.7K) — no major consistency flags.
⚠ Best value at the 2.5 alt line vs the 4.0 market main — be on the right number.

MODEL
signals · methodology · card guide · v9.9
Performance Tracker
through 2026-07-02

Live results by signal tier — updated each session. ROI uses actual FD odds. Bias = avg(actual − projected Ks), positive means model over-projects. MAE = mean absolute projection error in Ks.

■ Win rate bar fills to actual %▲ Bias positive = model tends to over-project Ks◆ MAE in strikeouts — lower is better● Bias within range
Signal Tiers — What Each Badge Means
STRONG · 2uHighest conviction. Model projects meaningfully more Ks than the line with strong probability and high confidence across multiple signals. Recommended 2 unit bet.
MOD · 1uModerate conviction. Clear edge vs the line with reasonable confidence. Standard 1 unit bet (1.5u when confidence is elevated and variance is low).
LEAN O · 0.5uDirectional lean over. Model sees an edge but confidence or probability does not clear the full action threshold. Half unit only.
LEAN U · 0.5uDirectional lean under. Model projects below the line with some confidence but not enough for full conviction. Half unit only.
UNDER · 1uHigh conviction fade. Model projects meaningfully fewer Ks than the line with strong confidence. 1 unit bet (1.5u when confidence is highest).
WATCH ⇑⇓Signal exists but does not clear the action threshold — confidence too low or variance too high. No bet recommended. Orange = over direction, Silver = under. Blocker reason shown on the card.
PASSNo meaningful signal. Model projects close to the line or insufficient data to form a view.
CAP — Model Confidence

CAP (0-100%) measures how aligned the signals are — not a win probability. High CAP means multiple independent signals point the same direction. Used to gate tier assignment — higher tiers require higher CAP.

Reading a Card
PROJ strip — model projection, edge vs line, P(over), CAP confidence
L10 form bar — last 10 starts coloured by above/below projection
Arsenal panel — pitch mix with whiff% and putaway%. P·WHF = pitcher whiff rate. OPP·WHF = lineup average whiff rate vs that pitch
Lineup panel — confirmed batting order with per-batter K% and whiff%. Green = pitcher advantage, Red = batter advantage
OPP K-MIX strip — BBRef season team K% vs MIX-K% weighted by this pitcher's specific pitch mix
Implied Odds box — probability of clearing each alt line as American odds. BET = recommended line, MKT = market main
Signal strip — action summary, unit size, key reason for signal
Data Sources
Live each session — pitcher L10 K counts, IP, ERA · FanDuel alt lines and odds · confirmed lineups
Savant CSV — batter K%/whiff%/xBA per pitch type · pitcher arsenal whiff%/FPS%/putaway%
Researched — team wRC+ by handedness · L10 runs/game · park factors · bullpen ERA · BBRef team K% splits
Not included — live Statcast splits · bullpen usage · real-time injury status beyond confirmed lineup
Hits TabBETA

P(≥1 hit) for each batter tonight. Built from confirmed lineups and pitcher arsenal data. Sorted by edge vs FanDuel's posted hit line. Early stage — no bullpen data or recent trends. Large edges often reflect model limitations, not market errors.

⚠ For informational and entertainment purposes only. Projections are model outputs — not guarantees. Past performance does not guarantee future results. Bet responsibly.

↑
Hit ProbabilityBETA

P(≥1 hit) from confirmed lineups × per-pitch xBA × starter arsenal. Sorted by FD edge. Large edges often reflect model gaps — no bullpen data, starter PAs only.

● Pitch-weighted○ Season fallback■ ≥+10pp■ +5–10pp⚠ Starter PAs only
